decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). The optimization history indicates diminishing returns from geometry changes and suggests shifting to material tuning. To further minimize the objective (chip_max_temperature), I will increase the heat spreader conductivity within the allowed 2.0x step ratio to enhance lateral heat spreading.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution, reducing the peak temperature gradient above the chip. This follows the strategy note to use material tuning when geometry changes stall.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
Slightly improved heat flux towards the side boundaries
risk_notes: Diminishing returns: At high conductivity values, the bottleneck may shift to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
Physical realism: 1000 W/(m*K) exceeds typical metal limits (approaching diamond/graphene ranges), though permitted by the variable bounds for this teaching case.
